description: Issue #205 Cat 4: TrueType and CFF font metrics extraction from embedded fonts

userStories.title: Parse TrueType hmtx table for horizontal glyph widths
userStories.description: Embedded TrueType fonts (/FontFile2) contain an 'hmtx' (horizontal metrics) table with per-glyph advance widths. Currently font_metrics.rs only uses /Widths arrays from the PDF font dictionary. For fonts where /Widths is incomplete or absent, parse the hmtx table from the embedded TrueType font data to obtain accurate glyph widths.
acceptanceCriteria: TrueType hmtx table is parsed from /FontFile2 stream data
Per-glyph advance widths from hmtx are used when /Widths is incomplete
multi_font.pdf (benchmark) chars F1 improves from 0.588 to >=0.75
No regression on Type1 or CIDFont metrics
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/font_metrics.rs, crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs. TrueType file format: starts with offset table (4 bytes scaler type, 2 bytes numTables, ...), then table directory entries (4-byte tag, checksum, offset, length). Find 'hmtx' table via directory. hmtx format: numOfLongHorMetrics longHorMetric records (each: u16 advanceWidth, i16 lsb), followed by i16 leftSideBearing entries. Also need 'hhea' table to get numOfLongHorMetrics and 'maxp' for numGlyphs. Do NOT add external font parsing crates - implement minimal TrueType parsing inline.

userStories.title: Parse CFF/Type1C font widths for embedded CFF fonts
userStories.description: Type1C (CFF/Compact Font Format) fonts embedded as /FontFile3 contain glyph widths in the CharStrings data. These fonts are used in many real-world PDFs. Implement minimal CFF parsing to extract per-glyph widths from the embedded font program.
acceptanceCriteria: CFF font data from /FontFile3 is parsed for glyph widths
No regression on Type1 or TrueType font metrics
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/font_metrics.rs. CFF format: Header → Name INDEX → Top DICT INDEX → String INDEX → Global Subr INDEX → CharStrings INDEX. Width is encoded in the CharString data as the first operand before drawing operators. CFF uses a compact binary encoding. Focus on extracting just the widths, not full outline data. Ref: Adobe Technical Note #5176 (CFF specification). This is complex - implement incrementally and handle parse failures gracefully.
